danger_signals.visual_form: strange extraterrestrial entities visible in first-person view
danger_signals.meaning: lethal threat; unknown aggressor type requiring elimination

danger_signals.visual_form: human-derived enemies with visible transformation markers
danger_signals.meaning: contamination threat; formerly friendly entities now dangerous

danger_signals.visual_form: environmental burn marks, scorched surfaces on warship interiors
danger_signals.meaning: prior combat occurred; structural/systems integrity compromised

danger_signals.visual_form: adversaries careening into player view unpredictably
convulsive movement of defeated spider-type creatures

safety_signals.visual_form: staircases or elevators leading to next level
safety_signals.meaning: progression opportunity; escape route from current danger zone

safety_signals.visual_form: all enemies eliminated in current area
safety_signals.meaning: temporary safety; area secured for advancement

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: heavily fortified central command building; military infrastructure aesthetic
player character equipment/commandos with specialty training

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: alien creatures as primary enemy type; biological/non-human appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: damage patterns on Europa-1 consistent with alien weaponry; unusual structural damage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: fire damage throughout environment; destroyed/damaged warship interiors; corpses and blood
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies actively hostile; unpredictable movement patterns; infected humans attacking

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: overheated machinery; damaged reactor areas; structural deterioration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: time pressure on mission; urgency reflected in level design and enemy density

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: transformed humans among enemy roster; alien presence in human spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: infected entities behave differently than pure aliens; hybrid threat states

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: no active enemies; calm environment state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can proceed freely to exit; no combat encounters until next level

environmental_storytelling.example: Fire damage visible on warship Europa-1 communicates prior intense combat and systemic failure of the ship
Three distinct areas (space warship, merchant freighter, fortified command building, sub-basements) establish progression through different military/commercial installations
Mix of alien creatures and transformed humans tells story of infection/contamination spreading through crew
Heavily fortified central command building vs. abandoned merchant freighter communicate different security states and abandon patterns

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: search for escaped survivors; carleen around corners unpredictably; active pursuit of player
attack on sight despite prior friendly status; indicate infection spreading
can sneak through levels; optional to eliminate all enemies or proceed directly to exit; choice reflects tactical flexibility
twitching body convulsions post-death; visible suffering indicates successful elimination

notes: Zero Tolerance uses first-person perspective to communicate threat proximity and immediacy. The warship setting (Europa-1) as primary environment establishes claustrophobic confined-space danger. Character permadeath system ('deceased' marking) creates persistent world-state consequence. Level design with labyrinthine layouts reinforces disorientation and isolation. The flamethrower weapon (noted in reviews) suggests anti-organic threat focus relevant to alien/infected enemies. Multiplayer connectivity via link cable extends faction/team dynamics not fully detailed in narrative sources. World-state centers on time-pressure (nuclear core breach countdown) creating environmental pressure beyond enemy encounters.
